description:
You are an IT-support technician who has been asked to close a small set of monitoring tickets.  
Three internal micro-services expose a tiny read-only “status” API that you must verify with curl, then store the consolidated result in a log file so that the on-call dashboard can pick it up.

Available mock API
1. URL: http://localhost:8000/auth/status.json
2. URL: http://localhost:8000/files/status.json
3. URL: http://localhost:8000/billing/status.json

Each endpoint answers with a single-line JSON object that looks like:
{ "service": "<name>", "status": "<state>" }

The mock HTTP data files already exist under /home/user/mock_api/ and you are free to serve them any way you like (e.g., python -m http.server 8000, a lightweight netcat listener, etc.).  You do NOT need root permissions for anything.

What you must deliver
Create a new plain-text log file at:  
/home/user/ticket_logs/service_checks.log

Log-file format that **will** be automatically validated:
• One line per service, in this exact order: auth, files, billing  
• Fields are separated by a single TAB character (ASCII 0x09).  
• Field-1 → lowercase service name (auth, files, billing)  
• Field-2 → the HTTP status code returned by curl (should be 200)  
• Field-3 → the value of the “status” key found inside the JSON body  
• No extra whitespace and each line ends with a UNIX newline (\n).

An example of the expected final content is therefore:

auth<TAB>200<TAB>ok\n  
files<TAB>200<TAB>ok\n  
billing<TAB>200<TAB>degraded\n

(where <TAB> represents an actual tab character).

Summary of what you will do (for your own planning; do NOT echo these in the terminal):
1. Serve the JSON files on port 8000.
2. Use 3–4 separate curl commands—one per service—to fetch each endpoint.
3. Extract the numeric HTTP code and the “status” value from the JSON.
4. Assemble the three tab-separated lines exactly as specified and write them to /home/user/ticket_logs/service_checks.log.

When the file exists and its contents match the specification, the tickets are considered resolved.
truth:
Pre-existing files on disk before the agent starts:
Directory: /home/user/mock_api/
   ├── auth/status.json     ->  { "service": "auth", "status": "ok" }
   ├── files/status.json    ->  { "service": "files", "status": "ok" }
   └── billing/status.json  ->  { "service": "billing", "status": "degraded" }

Directory: /home/user/ticket_logs/
   (initially empty; writable by the agent)

File that must exist when the agent finishes:
Path: /home/user/ticket_logs/service_checks.log  
Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline characters):

auth<TAB>200<TAB>ok\n
files<TAB>200<TAB>ok\n
billing<TAB>200<TAB>degraded\n
(where <TAB> is ASCII 0x09 and \n is ASCII 0x0A)

Nothing else in the file—no carriage returns, no extra blank lines.
name: task_000000_41de36eb
